calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Melbostad for petitioner Heddy Pilpel, who was also present (via Zoom).
David Pilpel, objector in Pro Per.
Sylvia Sossner, heir/interested party, was in attendance (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on Amended Spousal Property Petition.

The Court inquired whether petitioner planned on filing a new 850 petition. Mr. Melbostad stated they were not, as they were unable to obtain a stipulation from David Pilpel. Mr. Melbostad requested an order confirming that 50% of the Raymond James accounts belong to surviving spouse Hattie Pilpel as her community property share. He stated there was no dispute that the funds in the accounts were acquired during marriage.

David Pilpel reported that parties had engaged in multiple meet-and-confer sessions regarding the Raymond James accounts and broader family issues, but no final agreement was reached. He made reference to a new 850 petition filed in the related trust matter, and stated he had not been served with that petition and that it appeared that no hearing date was issued for the petition.

It was clarified that a new 850 petition had not been filed, only a supplement was filed in the related trust matter, on a petition that had already been adjudicated by Judge Moody.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant in part, only as to the surviving spouse's share of community property. The petition was otherwise denied.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Continued To Sep-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul H. Melbostad for Heddy Pilpel, Petitioner (via Zoom).
David Pilpel, Objector in Pro Per (via Zoom).

Mr. Melbostad informed the Court that Heddy Pilpel had agreed to a continuance, as requested by David Pilpel, to allow time for further discussions. He noted that he had also spoken with Mr. Pilpel's attorney, and a meeting was being arranged to address questions concerning the trust.

The Court acknowledged that a supplement had been filed in the related trust matter but advised counsel that, in order to proceed properly, a new petition would need to be filed with proper notice. Although that issue was not on calendar for the day, the Court provided this guidance to avoid future delays.

The Court inquired whether the discussions were expected to result in a stipulation. Mr. Melbostad indicated that was the goal and stated that Sylvia, the decedent's daughter and another heir, was also expected to stipulate. No additional heirs were identified.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to September 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow the parties time to meet and potentially reach a stipulation.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Continued To Jul-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Paul Melbostad, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Heddy Pilpel, via Zoom videoconference, Petitioner.

David Pilpel, in person, in propria persona, Objector.

Edward Sossner, via Zoom videoconference, interested party.

At the outset, the court confirms with David Pilpel that the Declaration he recently filed was intended to be an objection. Mr. Pilpel offers advocacy for his position. The court queries Mr. Melobostad as to why he did not refile an 850 Petition in the related Trust matter. Mr. Melbostad offers an update regarding his motives. The court advises that it is inclined to grant the instant petition only as it pertains to confirming the surviving spouse's share of the community property. The parties presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the court's tentative ruling. At the conclusion of argument, the court queries Mr. Pilpel whether he would be amenable to signing a consent to place the funds related to this matter to the related trust matter. Mr. Pilpel advises that he might be willing to do so but would like a chance to consider the preceding. After additional discussion, the court advises that it remains inclined to take the matter under submission and rule as previously stated or, continue this matter to allow the parties to meet and confer regarding the consent. At the court's query, Mr. Melbostad advises that he would be willing to work with Mr. Pilpel to resolve this matter as discussed.

After discussion regarding a continuance date, the subject matter is continued to Jul-15-2025 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Should the parties agree to transfer the above-mentioned funds to the related Trust matter, via Mr. Pilpel's consent, the court instructs the parties to file a stipulation one (1) week prior to the next calling.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Chwin. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Continued To Jun-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Paul Melbostad appeared vi zoom for and with Heddy Pilpel (petitioner).
David Pilpel appeared via zoom (pro per) (objector).

Discussion was held regarding decedent's community property interest to petitioner, that her son is not cooperating or communicating with his mother, that he is residing at the property located at 383 Crestlake Dr. in San Francisco, and that he doesn't pay for anything.

The Court takes David Pilpel's position as an objection to the spousal property petition. The objector to file a written objection by June 3, 2025. The matter is continued to June 10, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Thompson. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
